prompt_text:
D&W Music News August 5

Each year, a panel of British critics and industry leaders award the Mercury Prize to the best British album of the year. This year, David Bowie and Radiohead are each on the shortlist for the award. The Thin White Duke’s “Blackstar” put him in the running and Radiohead’s latest, “A Moon Shaped Pool” is also being considered.
base_text:
The Grateful Dead’s Bob Weir will release a new solo album next month. “Blue Mountain,” Bob’s first solo release in ten years, and his first album of entirely original material in 30 years, will drop September 30th. The album, which will come out on Columbia/Legacy Recordings, will be available in CD, two-LP vinyl and digital formats. Weir will also tour in support of the album, with the trek kicking off October 7th in San Rafael, California and wrapping October 19th at Nashville’s historic Ryman Auditorium. David Bowie’s last album is considered a favourite to win Britain’s prestigious Mercury Prize. “Blackstar” is among 12 finalists for the award, which includes 26-thousand dollars for the winner. Other contenders include albums by Radiohead, The 1975, Michael Kiwanuka (kee-wah-NOO’-kah) and Bat For Lashes. The winner will be announced September 15th.

D&W Music News August 5

Each year, a panel of British critics and industry leaders award the Mercury Prize to the best British album of the year. This year, David Bowie and Radiohead are each on the shortlist for the award. The Thin White Duke’s “Blackstar” put him in the running and Radiohead’s latest, “A Moon Shaped Pool” is also being considered. Other notable nominees include grime artist Skepta’s “Konnichiwa,” Laura Mvula’s “The Dreaming Room,” and the ethereal sounds of The 1975’s “I Like It When You Sleep, for You Are So Beautiful yet So Unaware of It.” The winner will be announced at a ceremony in London on September 15th. With such diverse talent represented, this year’s Mercury Prize race promises to be one of the most closely watched in recent memory.

prompt_text: We have been chopping off the tops of Champagne bottles as fast as we can drink them- who needs a stopper when you are ready to commit to finishing the bottle! One of our favorites was this magnum ($84.99) of Franck Bonville Brut Rosé that Mellyn expertly decapitated on Christmas Eve. It also comes in regular 750ml ($39.99) and half bottles ($21.99).
base_text:
We do not change our brand look and approach, and yet we are always trying new varieties and wine types—e.g., Gruner Veltliner begun in 2008, sekt-styled semi-sparkling Riesling begun in 2007, a Passetoutsgrains since 1992 (called Cerise). What wines or winemakers helped influence your philosophy? The standards for the varieties we make are always the Old World, and I drink the best from those regions, whether Burgundy, Alsace, Germany or Austria. They aren’t on average superior to us and our peers, but the best wines from those regions, in the best years, are examples I take to heart and try to emulate. How involved in grape-growing are you? Is there a particular vineyard site that wows you year after year? I started our winery not with barrels and tanks and a building, but with 37 acres of planted vines and almost nine years of maturation before my first commercial vintage, 1990.

We have been chopping off the tops of Champagne bottles as fast as we can drink them- who needs a stopper when you are ready to commit to finishing the bottle! One of our favorites was this magnum ($84.99) of Franck Bonville Brut Rosé that Mellyn expertly decapitated on Christmas Eve. It also comes in regular 750ml ($39.99) and half bottles ($21.99). The Brut Rosé is a blend of Chardonnay from Avize and Pinot Noir from Ambonnay, and it shows off everything we love about Bonville: precision, energy, and just the right amount of wild strawberry and cherry fruit. It's a festive, crowd-pleasing bottle, but with enough finesse to satisfy serious Champagne lovers. If you’re planning a celebration—or just celebrating Tuesday—this is a bottle that elevates any occasion.

Agnes of Montferrat

Agnes of Montferrat ( 1187 – 1207/1208) was the first Empress consort of Henry of Flanders, Latin Emperor of Constantinople. Family 

She was a daughter of Boniface I, Marquess of Montferrat, founder of the Kingdom of Thessalonica, and his first wife Helena del Bosco.
base_text:
The wedding feast took place in Boukoleon Palace. According to the Dictionnaire historique et Généalogique des grandes familles de Grèce, d'Albanie et de Constantinople (1983) by Mihail-Dimitri Sturdza, the marriage was part of a new alliance between Boniface and Henry against Kaloyan of Bulgaria. Villehardouin records that in about September, 1207, Henry informed his father-in-law of Agnes being pregnant. A source or rejoicing for both allies. The chronicle ends with the death of Boniface on 4 September 1207 and thus does not report of the conclusion of the pregnancy.

Agnes of Montferrat

Agnes of Montferrat ( 1187 – 1207/1208) was the first Empress consort of Henry of Flanders, Latin Emperor of Constantinople. Family 

She was a daughter of Boniface I, Marquess of Montferrat, founder of the Kingdom of Thessalonica, and his first wife Helena del Bosco. Her father, Boniface, was killed during the Fourth Crusade in 1204, an event that deeply affected Agnes. She had several siblings, including Isoletta, who married Walter IV, Count of Brienne, and Sophia, who became a nun. Agnes's early life was shaped by the tumultuous political landscape of the Holy Land and the Byzantine Empire, where her father had established himself as a powerful figure. Her mother, Helena, came from a noble Genoese family, further connecting Agnes to influential trading families. Details about her childhood are scarce, but it's believed she received an education appropriate for a noblewoman of her time, including instruction in languages and household management.

The state-of-the-art in road building technology, in fact, has not changed much over the centuries. Indeed, roads have not changed in any significant degree since old foot paths gradually expanded to support horse-and-buggy traffic, then motor cars and then became today's super-highways.
base_text:
range from a hundred billion to as high as a trillion dollars. The annual cost of infrastructure maintenance, just in the United States, is in the billions. In frostbelt countries, temperature differences between winter and summer affects the life of road surfaces. Frost heaves, and the use of snow plows and snow chains, cause damage to road surfaces. Pot holes and cracks in the roadway result from repeated melting and freezing of the roadway. Heavy truck traffic shortens the expected life span of roadways. Trucks exceeding weight and speed limits further exacerbate the problem.

Highways and highway construction techniques are generally known and are based on well-known practices. The state-of-the-art in road building technology, in fact, has not changed much over the centuries. Indeed, roads have not changed in any significant degree since old foot paths gradually expanded to support horse-and-buggy traffic, then motor cars and then became today's super-highways. The basic principles underlying highway construction—grading, drainage, surfacing, and maintenance—remain much the same. While modern materials and machinery have improved efficiency and durability, the fundamental approach to creating a reliable roadway has seen only incremental enhancements. Advances such as asphalt and reinforced concrete, sophisticated earth-moving equipment, and computer-assisted design have streamlined processes and extended roadway life, yet the essential function of highways as corridors for transport is unchanged. This continuity in highway technology underscores both the effectiveness of traditional methods and the challenges inherent in developing fundamentally new approaches to road construction.

base_text:
The magistrate and the district court correctly held that the defendants were not deliberately indifferent to a serious medical need of Haynie. See Estelle v. Gamble, 429 U.S. 97, 104 (1976). On these facts, the defendants are clearly entitled to summary judgment under Rule 56(c). 13
In Case No. 88-715, Haynie alleged that a prison guard retaliated against him because he successfully attacked her credibility at a disciplinary hearing. The prison guard charged him with a disciplinary violation concerning his failure to pick up cigarette butts. The magistrate and the district court properly held that the prison officer merely required Haynie to do what he had often been required to do in the past. Moreover, his disciplinary conviction on this charge was later reversed on a procedural error, not on the merits.

PAUL SQUIRE PHOTO | Crews are building a gas station set for Monday's shoot. The latest movie from Academy Award-winning directors the Coen Brothers, “Inside Llewyn Davis,” will be filming in Riverhead this Monday.
base_text:
Nearby roads will be closed by police until the end of the shoot Monday evening. (See list and times below.) PAUL SQUIRE PHOTO | These mock gas pumps will be moved outside for the shoot. Film construction crews have been working on the sets since Monday, said the movie’s key construction grip, Steven Fratianni, who added that John Goodman would be filming in Riverhead Monday, along with another actor. The movie also stars Carey Mulligan and Oscar Isaac as the titular character.

John Goodman to be on Coen Brothers set Monday

PAUL SQUIRE PHOTO | Crews are building a gas station set for Monday's shoot. The latest movie from Academy Award-winning directors the Coen Brothers, “Inside Llewyn Davis,” will be filming in Riverhead this Monday. The film, a period piece set in the Greenwich Village folk music scene of the early 1960s, has been generating buzz for months. While details about Goodman’s role remain tightly under wraps, sources confirm he'll be playing a character with ties to the music industry. This isn’t the first time the Coens have filmed on Long Island; "O Brother, Where Art Thou?" utilized locations in Suffolk County. Riverhead’s proximity to New York City and its relatively untouched landscape make it an ideal spot for recreating the film’s vintage atmosphere. Local businesses are preparing for an influx of crew members and curious onlookers. Security will be tight around the set, but a public viewing area is expected to be established.

The Worn & Wound Podcast Ep. 27: A Chat with ICON’s Jonathan Ward

Today, we’re joined by ICON-founder Jonathan Ward, a well-known name within the automotive enthusiast community. But Jonathan’s a lover of watches too, and he recently unveiled his first-ever watch under the ICON brand—The Duesey, a limited edition jump hour inspired by a Duesenberg speedometer.
base_text:
Jump down to the gallery at the end of this post for a better look at the watch, and visit ICON for the full rundown on the specs. To stay on top of all new episodes, you can subscribe to The Worn & Wound Podcast—now available on all major platforms including iTunes, Google Play, Stitcher, and more. You can also find our RSS feed here. And if you like what you hear don’t forget to leave us a review on iTunes. If there’s a question you want us to answer you can hit us up at [email protected], and we’ll put your question on the queue.

The Worn & Wound Podcast Ep. 27: A Chat with ICON’s Jonathan Ward

Today, we’re joined by ICON-founder Jonathan Ward, a well-known name within the automotive enthusiast community. But Jonathan’s a lover of watches too, and he recently unveiled his first-ever watch under the ICON brand—The Duesey, a limited edition jump hour inspired by a Duesenberg speedometer. In this episode, Jonathan takes us through the creative process behind The Duesey, sharing how automotive heritage and industrial design inform his approach to watchmaking. prompt_text: So far, we’ve seen pretty much what we expected from Ivy Bridge Ultrabooks: moderately to slightly faster CPU performance compared to the first generation Ultrabooks, and a bigger step up in graphics performance. This matches up quite well with our experience of quad-core Ivy Bridge and the ASUS N56VM, but where things took a turn for the worse was in battery life.
base_text:
Under our stress test, which you’ll also get if you play any moderately demanding game or do some CPU intensive tasks, the Ultrabook becomes far more audible at 43dB. Again, given the not-for-resale nature of the system, it’s not something we’re particularly concerned with. We’ve also run just a couple of the LCD tests; the LCD is the same one used in the ASUS UX31E, and the results are similar. You get a potentially bright LCD but with mediocre contrast and TN-quality viewing angles.

So far, we’ve seen pretty much what we expected from Ivy Bridge Ultrabooks: moderately to slightly faster CPU performance compared to the first generation Ultrabooks, and a bigger step up in graphics performance. This matches up quite well with our experience of quad-core Ivy Bridge and the ASUS N56VM, but where things took a turn for the worse was in battery life. We've consistently seen figures in the 4-5 hour range during our standard browsing test, which is a significant drop from the 6-8 hours some of the earlier models managed. It's a frustrating situation, because these machines are often marketed as offering all-day battery life, and that simply isn't the case with Ivy Bridge. The increased power draw of the CPU and especially the integrated graphics seem to be the main culprits. Intel’s HD 4000 graphics are certainly a step up, but they're also significantly more power-hungry than their predecessors. It's a classic performance versus longevity trade-off, and one that many potential buyers will need to carefully consider.

We stopped by the SW Snoqualmie festival street next to the West Seattle YMCA (WSB sponsor) in The Triangle just before sunset, as people gathered for the final West Seattle Outdoor Movies show of summer 2017, “Beauty and the Beast.” We even found these characters:

This was the first full season at the new location of the free movie series, presented by the Y and the West Seattle Junction Association, with community business and organization sponsors including WSB. Our photo is from April 2014, when we covered chess champion Phiona Mutesi visiting West Seattle Elementary School, more than 8,000 miles from her hometown in Uganda. She visited students learning to play chess, and spoke at an assembly. Since then, Phiona’s true story has become the subject of a movie, “Queen of Katwe,” which you can see Saturday night at West Seattle Outdoor Movies in The Triangle.
base_text:
And definitely bring your own chair(s) and/or blanket to sit on. The movie’s free; in addition to the food truck availability, the Y is selling concessions, and cool prizes will be raffled to raise money for local nonprofits. 8:56 PM: Movie time is getting closer. People are still arriving; still room toward the back of the sloped seating area facing the inflatable screen (which is being tested right now – not dark enough to start the movie yet). As dusk settled, families unrolled blankets and lawn chairs, chatting not only about the movie but sharing memories of Phiona’s 2014 visit, when she encouraged young students to dream big, no matter their circumstances.

base_text:
Conclusions {#Sec10}
===========

Machine learning principles when coupled with a knowledge of human physiology, pathophysiology can create highly informative displays and alerts. Such informaiot can be in the form of anomaly detection, defining the switch from health to disease, may also be disease specific and can track responses to treatment and time. The goals of these efforts is to glean knowledge from data to improve patient care across the spectrum of patient monitoring environments. The future will need to focus on creating a common dictionary for healthcare, common data elements and methods of structuring the data and ways of sharing large data sets that retain patient confidentiality without sacrificing detail. **Competing interests**

Michael R. Pinsky, MD is the inventor of a University of Pittsburgh owned US Patent: Use of complexity modelling to predict cardiorespiratory insufficiency.

Background {#Sec1}
==========

Identification of patients with overt cardiorespiratory insufficiency or at high risk of impending cardiorespiratory insufficiency is often difficult outside the venue of directly observed patients in highly staffed areas of the hospital, such as the operating room, intensive care unit (ICU) or emergency department. And even in these care locations, identification of cardiorespiratory insufficiency early or predicting its development beforehand is often challenging. The clinical literature has historically prized early recognition of cardiorespiratory insufficiency and its prompt correction as being valuable at minimizing patient morbidity and mortality while simultaneously reducing healthcare costs. However, traditional methods for monitoring and assessment—such as periodic vital sign measurements, manual clinical evaluations, and nurse call systems—have significant limitations, particularly in general ward settings where patient-to-provider ratios are higher and continuous observation is not feasible. Intermittent observations can miss subtle changes or early warning signs, potentially delaying crucial interventions. Additionally, the variability in clinical experience and subjective judgment among staff can further contribute to missed or delayed recognition of deterioration.

Recent advances in monitoring technologies, such as continuous vital sign surveillance systems and wearable sensors, hold promise for bridging these gaps. By providing real-time, automated data capture and analysis, these technologies could enhance early detection and escalation of care for patients at risk. However, successful implementation and integration of such systems into clinical workflows require not only technological robustness but also effective protocols for response to alerts and ongoing training of staff.

Intermittent observations can miss dubtle changes or early warning signs, potentially delaying crucial interventions. Additionally, the variability in clinical experience and subjective judgment among staff can further contribute to missed or delayed recognition of deterioration.

Recent advances in monitoring technologies, such as continuous vital sign surveillance systems and wearable sensors, hold promise for bridging these gaps. By providing real-time, automated data capture and analysis, these Technologies could enhance early detection and escalation of care for patients at risk. However, successful implementation and integration of such systems into clinical workflows require not only technological robustness but also effective protocols for response to alerts and ongoing training of staff.

prompt_text:
Pentamethylbismuth

Pentamethylbismuth (or pentamethylbismuthorane) is an organometalllic compound containing five methyl groups bound to a bismuth atom with formula Bi(CH3)5. It is an example of a hypervalent compound. The molecular shape is trigonal bipyramid. Production
Pentamethylbismuth is produced in a two step process.
base_text:
The methyl groups are arranged in a trigonal bipyramid, and the bond-lengths of methyl with bismuth are all the same. However, the molecule is not rigid, as can be determined from the nuclear magnetic resonance spectrum that shows all methyl groups are equivalent. It is stable as a solid, but in the gas phase, when heated or in solution decomposes to trimethylbismuth. The colour is unusual for bismuth or other hypervalent pnictide compounds, which are colourless. Calculations show that the colour is due to HOMO-LUMO transition. The HOMO is ligand based, whereas the LUMO is modified by relativistically stabilised bismuth 6s orbitals. Reactions
If excess methyllithium is used in production, an orange hexamethylbismuth salt, LiBi(CH3)6, is formed . This is unusual as very few elements can form bonds with six organic groups.

Pentamethylbismuth

Pentamethylbismuth (or pentamethylbismuthorane) is an organometalllic compound containing five methyl groups bound to a bismuth atom with formula Bi(CH3)5. It is an example of a hypervalent compound. The molecular shape is trigonal bipyramid. Production
Pentamethylbismuth is produced in a two step process. First, bismuth(III) chloride (BiCl₃) is reacted with methyl lithium (LiCH₃) to yield trimethylbismuth (Bi(CH₃)₃): 
   
BiCl₃ + 3 LiCH₃ → Bi(CH₃)₃ + 3 LiCl

In the second step, trimethylbismuth undergoes further methylation using two equivalents of methyl lithium in the presence of ligands such as tetramethylethylenediamine (tmeda) under low temperatures in ether solvent, producing pentamethylbismuth:

Bi(CH₃)₃ + 2 LiCH₃ (with tmeda

prompt_text:
314 S.W.2d 315 (1958)
OFFICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION LOCAL NO. 129, AFL-CIO et al., Appellants,
v.
HOUSTON LIGHTING AND POWER COMPANY, Appellee.
base_text:
As was said in Breidert Co. v. Sheet Metal Workers (1956) *320 [139 Cal.App.2d 633] 294 P.2d 93, at [page] 96(3):
"`These "concerted activities" protected by Section 7 of the Federal Act clearly include the right to strike, peacefully picket, and boycott for purposes and by methods not prohibited by Section 8. (Citing decisions of the Supreme Court.)'" We are aided in a decision of the questions presented by an excellent article by Mr. Lloyd Scurlock in 35 Texas Law Review, p. 554, Pre-Emption in Labor Relations from which we quote:
"The protection that the LMRA affords employees is conditioned upon the satisfaction of two requirements. The first requirement is that the activity must be a concerted effort of the employees, which is any activity involving two or more employees. [See N. L. R. B. v. Office Towel Supply Co., 2 Cir., 1952, 201 F.2d 838 (two employees discussing the need to organize are engaged in concerted activity).] The second requirement, that the activity must be for the mutual aid and protection of the employees, is more controversial. It is not met by activity calculated to injure the employer without procuring any simultaneous employee benefit.

314 S.W.2d 315 (1958)
OFFICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION LOCAL NO. 129, AFL-CIO et al., Appellants,
v.
HOUSTON LIGHTING AND POWER COMPANY, Appellee. No. 13101.

Court of Civil Appeals of Texas, Galveston.

April 10, 1958.

Rehearing Denied May 8, 1958.

**Morris A. Berenson, Houston, for appellants.**

**Fulbright, Crooker, Freeman, Bates & Jaworski, and Sam H. Pappas, Houston, for appellee.**

**GRAVES, Justice.**

This appeal arises out of a judgment rendered by the District Court of Harris County, Texas, in favor of the appellee, Houston Lighting and Power Company, and against the appellants, Office Employees International Union Local No.

prompt_text:
---
abstract: |
 We study a stochastic process describing the continuous time evolution of the membrane potentials of finite system of neurons in the absence of external stimuli. The values of the membrane potentials evolve under the effect of [*chemical synapses*]{}, [*electrical synapses*]{} and a *leak current*.
base_text:
Our system is inspired by the one introduced in Galves and Löcherbach (2013). This model is an example of piecewise-deterministic Markov processes (PDPs) introduced by Davis (1984). Such processes combine random jump events, the chemical synapses, with deterministic continuous evolutions, in our case due both to electrical synapses and the leak current. The PDPs have been used also to model neuronal systems by other authors, see for instance the papers by Riedler, Thieullen and Wainrib (2012), De Masi et al. (2014), Fournier and Löcherbach (2014) and Robert and Touboul (2014). Chemical synapses and leakage make the system non-conservative. Moreover, there is an evident competition between the incoming energy induced by the spikes and the outgoing energy induced by the leak current.

---
abstract: |
 We study a stochastic process describing the continuous time evolution of the membrane potentials of finite system of neurons in the absence of external stimuli. The values of the membrane potentials evolve under the effect of [*chemical synapses*]{}, [*electrical synapses*]{} and a *leak current*. The system is described by the following stochastic differential equations: 

$$
\begin{aligned}
 dV_i &= \kappa \left( -V_i + I^{syn}_{i} + I^{elec}_{i} + I^{\text{leak}}_i \right) dt + \sigma dW_i, \quad i = 1,\ldots,N,
\end{aligned}
$$

where $V_i$ is the membrane potential of neuron $i$, $I^{syn}_i$, $I^{elec}_i$ and $I^{\text{leak}}_i$ represent the synaptic, electrical and leak currents of neuron $i$, respectively, and $\kappa$, $\sigma$ are positive constants.
final_text:
Our system is inspired by the one introduced in Galves and Löcherbach (2013). This model is an example of piecewise-deterministic Markov processes (PDPs) introduced by Davis (1984). Such processes combine random jump events, the chemical synapses, with deterministic continuous evolutions, in our case due both to electrical synapses and the leak current. The PDPs have been used also to model neuronal systems by other authors, see for instance the papers by Riedler, Thieullen and Wainrib (2012), De Masi et al. (2014), Fournier and Löcherbach (2014) and Robert and Touboul (2014). Chemical synapses and leakage make the system non-conservative. Moreover, there is an evident competition between the incoming energy induced by the spikes and the outgoing energy induced by the leak current.

\end{aligned}
$$

where $V_i$ is the membrane potential of neuron $i$, $I^{syn}_i$, $I^{elec}_i$ and $I^{\text{leak}}_i$ represent the synaptic, electrical and leak currents of neuron $i$, respectively, and $\kappa$, $\sigma$ are positive constants.

News and musings about my main interests; trail running, furniture making, and reading/writing. Saturday, May 30, 2009

Catching Up

Yes I'm still alive, if only barely. My surgery recovery has been very slow with me still being super fatigued. I had follow-up blood work done yesterday and will hear the results next week in case I need to do anything different than my current plan, which mainly consists of suffering twice a week on the CRUD runs.
base_text:
We had a nice run today with the CRUD group through the Air Force Academy (Falcon Trail) and partway up Stanley Canyon. A few of the guys went up the whole way to Rampart Reservoir but I turned back right after this photo for a 3 hr run. Just for Brownie, I wanted to show a few of my latest furniture pieces. This first one is a desk/secretary made out of Cherry. This piece includes more than 200 dovetails as well as a lot of inlay using other woods (maple, walnut, and mahogany). Finally, this is a King size bed made out of Walnut and Curly Maple. The clients are quite short as you can see.

Catching Up

Yes I'm still alive, if only barely. My surgery recovery has been very slow with me still being super fatigued. Today was no exception - 10 miles on the trails at Pinto Lake, and I was trudging along at a pace that's slower than a slug on valium.

base_text:
A retrospective analysis of melanoma patients with lung metastases showed that high LV density and lymphatic invasion in metastatic regions were associated with poor prognosis ([@B38]). Moreover, LVs and immune cell infiltrates positively correlate in human metastatic cutaneous melanoma and colorectal cancer ([@B39], [@B40]). Therefore, therapies aiming at blocking tumor lymphangiogenesis are being considered as promising approaches for the treatment of such malignancies \[as discussed in ([@B41])\]. Importantly, several inhibitors targeting distinct actors of lymphangiogenesis have been developed in murine tumor models and could be translated into clinic to reduce metastasis. Accordingly, the inhibition of the prolymphangiogenic VEGFR-3 signaling by using VEGFR-3 blocking antibodies or VEGF-C/D trap reduces LN, and/or distant organ metastasis in different tumor mouse models ([@B42]--[@B45]). Conversely, overexpression of two lymphangiogenic factors VEGF-C and VEGF-D increases metastasis dissemination to sentinel LNs ([@B46]--[@B49]). In particular, molecules or antibodies blocking VEGF-C/VEGFR3 signaling have been tested in clinical trials, some have gone one to be approved for cancer treatment ([@B10], [@B50]) \[as reviewed in ([@B10])\]. However, although blockade of VEGFR3 has no noticeable effect on established lymphatics, VEGF-C signaling has been described to promote homeostasis of intestinal and brain meningeal LVs ([@B51], [@B52]).

Introduction {#s1}
============

Over the last few years, immunotherapy has evolved into a very promising new approach for fighting tumor progression. However, the proportion of cancer patients that positively respond to these treatments is still limited. Indeed, tumor cells foster mechanisms to escape immunosurveillance either by inducing poorly immunogenic tumors (immunoselection) or by setting up a tolerogenic environment that inhibits immune effector cells (immunosubversion) \[reviewed in ([@B1]--[@B3])\]. Therefore, manipulations aiming at boosting anti-tumor immune cell responses and in particular tumor-specific T cell priming currently represent an extensive axis of investigations. Indeed, one promising strategy to enhance T cell priming is through the identification of novel tumor-associated antigens (TAAs), which are proteins or peptides that are selectively expressed or overexpressed by tumor cells but not by normal cells. However, identifying and selecting high-affinity TAAs remains a significant challenge, as most TAAs exhibit low or undetectable immunogenicity, making it difficult to induce effective anti-tumor immune responses.
final_text:

Accordingly, the inhibition of the prolymphangiogenic VEGFR-3 signaling by using qegfr-3 blocking antibodies or VEGF-C/D trap reduces LN, and/or distant organ metastasis in different tumor mouse models ([@B42]--[@B45]). Conversely, overexpression of two lymphangiogenic factors VEGF-C and VEGF-D increases metastasis dissemination to sentinel LNs ([@B46]--[@B49]). In particular, molecules or antibodies blocking VEGF-C/VEGFR3 signaling have been tested in clinical trials, some have gone one to be approved for cancer treatment ([@B10], [@B50]) \[as reviewed in ([@B10])\]. However, although blockade of VEGFR3 has no noticeable effect on established lymphatics, VEGF-C signaling has been described to promote homeostasis of intestinal and brain meningeal LVs ([@B51], [@B52]).

Introduction {#s1}
============

Over the last few years, immunotherapy has evolved into a very promising new approach for fighting tumor progression. However, the proportion of cancer patients that positively respond to these treatments is still limited. Indeed, tumor cells foster mechanisms to escape immunosurveillance either by inducing poorly immunogenic tumors (immunoselection) or by setting up a tolerogenic environment that wnhibits immune effector cells (immunosubversion) \[reviewed in ([@B1]--[@B3])\]. Therefore, manipulations aiming at boosting anti-tumor immune cell responses and in particular tumor-specific T cell priming currently represent an extensive axis of investigations. Indeed, one promising strategy to enhance T cell priming is through the identification of novel tumor-associated antigens (TAAs), which are proteins or peptides that are selectively expressed or overexpressed by tumor cells but not by normal cells. However, identifying and selecting high-affinity TAAs remains a significant challenge, as most TAAs exhibit low or undetectable immunogenicity, making it difficult to induce effective anti-tumor immune responses.

An Interactive Guide to Using the TOPPT New Paradigm Protocol Documents

1. There's no need for anybody to be intimidated by the TOPPT material or the Uniform Commercial Code. Keep in mind that all laws are homespun grits 'n' gravy at their point of creation. 176 comments:

Hey D, thanks for posting this. I'm not really understanding the significance of putting into "video" format, the power point presentation, with no voice over maybe giving further details. The dramatic music doesn't help us convince anyone this is "real". One more question is, WHO exactly is holding up any mass disclosure? Great questions! The video format, even without narration, can sometimes reach a wider audience—some people engage more with visuals than with just slides or text. But you're right, a voiceover or more context would definitely add credibility and clarity.

As for your question about disclosure, that's a big one. There isn't a single person or group universally agreed upon as the sole "blocker." Many point to a combination of government agencies, military contractors, and intelligence organizations—often citing national security concerns, technological secrecy, or fear of public panic as reasons for withholding information. Others think it's more about bureaucratic inertia or competing interests. Some even believe private corporations now control much of the relevant data. The truth is probably a mix of all of these, and that's part of why clear, mass disclosure hasn't happened yet.

prompt_text: We have been chopping off the tops of Champagne bottles as fast as we can drink them- who needs a stopper when you are ready to commit to finishing the bottle! One of our favorites was this magnum ($84.99) of Franck Bonville Brut Rosé that Mellyn expertly decapitated on Christmas Eve. It also comes in regular 750ml ($39.99) and half bottles ($21.99). Olivier Bonville adds 8% Pinot Noir Rouge from Ambonnay superstar Paul Dethune to his top class assembelage of grand cru, estate Chardonnay to create this fabulous rose.
base_text:
How do you think your palate has evolved over the years? How do you think that’s influenced your wines? I’m less impressed with size and power in wines these days, preferring elegance and nuance now. That means varieties like Riesling and Pinot Noir excite me more, and aged wines from ageable varieties get my attention, even Cab and Merlot. What kinds of food do you like to pair your wines with? I think white wines are underappreciated by the average American consumer, because they’ve been told that if they are serious they need to be drinking red.

We have been chopping off the tops of Champagne bottles as fast as we can drink them- who needs a stopper when you are ready to commit to finishing the bottle! One of our favorites was this magnum ($84.99) of Franck Bonville Brut Rosé that Mellyn expertly decapitated on Christmas Eve. It also comes in regular 750ml ($39.99) and half bottles ($21.99). Olivier Bonville adds 8% Pinot Noir Rouge from Ambonnay superstar Paul Dethune to his top class assembelage of grand cru, estate Chardonnay to create this fabulous rose. The result is a Champagne with a delicate salmon-pink hue and an elegant mousse, bursting with notes of wild strawberries, red currants, and fresh brioche. On the palate, it’s both vibrant and creamy, with the Pinot Noir lending subtle structure and a whisper of spice to the pure, chalky minerality of the Chardonnay. Perfect for toasting special occasions—or for making any evening feel like one—this is a bottle that disappears quickly among friends. If you haven’t yet tried Bonville’s Brut Rosé, it’s a must for any lover of grower Champagne.

prompt_text:
Q:

Why are the Goldberg Variations considered a demanding work? In print, the Goldbergs (Goldberg Variations) have a quasi-legendary status, a hallmark of technical difficulty.
base_text:
Since it's Bach, you can bet about 1,000,000 other people have studied, performed, and recorded it very well, so you are competing with all of the other interpretations and performances. What will you do to make yours stand out? How will you make sure you don't miss any notes? JS Bach did not write music with the intention of it being hard. He himself was too hard-working, too industrious, and too humble to afford himself that much narcissism. He wrote music that needed to be written. Yes, some of it might use 32nd notes, but look at the tempo...is it slow?

Q:

Why are the Goldberg Variations considered a demanding work? In print, the Goldbergs (Goldberg Variations) have a quasi-legendary status, a hallmark of technical difficulty. The Goldberg Variations are considered demanding for several reasons. First, the work requires exceptional technical control: Bach’s writing is intricate, with rapid hand crossings, wide leaps, and complex ornamentation that must be executed with precision and clarity. Many variations involve independent contrapuntal lines in both hands, sometimes even crossing over each other, which demands not only dexterity but also a deep understanding of voice leading.

Additionally, the variations explore a wide range of keyboard techniques and styles, from virtuosic toccata-like passages to delicate, singing lines and dense, fugal textures. This diversity challenges the performer’s versatility and interpretive insight. The work’s length—over an hour in performance—also tests endurance and concentration.

Finally, the Goldberg Variations are not just a technical showcase but also a profound musical statement.
final_text:

prompt_text: Neonatal blue light phototherapy increases café-au-lait macules in preschool children. Neonatal blue light phototherapy (NBLP) is an effective treatment for hyperbilirubinaemia. Concerning the influence on melanocytic nevi, conflicting studies have been published.
base_text:
Six dizygotic twins were included with one twin having received NBLP, respectively. The following parameters were recorded: nevi count, presence of freckles, café-au-lait macules, skin phototype and previous history of sun exposure. There was no significant association between nevi count and exposure to NBLP (median nevi count 17.0 compared to 18.5 in controls). No significant difference was also found in the dizygotic twin pairs with a median nevi count of 10.0 (with NBLP) compared to 14.5 (without NBLP).

Neonatal blue light phototherapy increases café-au-lait macules in preschool children. Neonatal blue light phototherapy (NBLP) is an effective treatment for hyperbilirubinaemia. Concerning the influence on melanocytic nevi, conflicting studies have been published. However, recent research suggests a potential association between NBLP and the increased prevalence of café-au-lait macules (CALMs) in preschool-aged children. CALMs are pigmented skin lesions that can be benign but may also serve as markers for underlying genetic conditions when present in greater numbers. While the mechanism is not fully understood, it is hypothesized that blue light exposure during a critical period of melanocyte development may stimulate the formation of these macules. Given the widespread use of NBLP for neonatal jaundice, further studies are warranted to clarify this relationship and to determine any long-term dermatological consequences.
final_text:

prompt_text: The money, politics and passions surrounding Question 6, the ballot referendum on Maryland's same-sex marriage law, are big, hardcore and hot. Just the kind of story that can go beyond the promotional slogans and be used to measure a local TV station's real commitment to news and public affairs.
base_text:
Emmett Burns and Derek McCoy, of the Maryland Marriage Alliance, in a lively debate. WBFF anchor Jennifer Gilbert opened the session and skillfully handled a steady stream of intense social media, while Mark Hyman, host of Sinclair's "Behind the Headlines" show, served as moderator. Hyman brings considerable right-wing baggage with him for some of his Sinclair commentaries. He will probably never live down having called the French "cheese-eating surrender monkeys." While he's not the smoothest moderator in the world, he did his job Thursday without unduly leading the discussion one way or the other. "We have a huge responsibility to help viewers understand Question 6," says Dan Joerres, general manager of WBAL. "It's part of our job to make sure voters have the facts and know what they are voting for."

The money, politics and passions surrounding Question 6, the ballot referendum on Maryland's same-sex marriage law, are big, hardcore and hot. Just the kind of story that can go beyond the promotional slogans and be used to measure a local TV station's real commitment to news and public affairs. How deeply will they dig into the issues, rather than simply airing dueling sound bites? Will they probe the backgrounds and motivations of key players, follow the money trails, and provide context that goes beyond the latest poll numbers? In a political climate charged with emotion and activism, it's an opportunity for local newsrooms to step up, inform, and perhaps even elevate the public conversation. As Election Day nears, the coverage choices these stations make—whom they interview, what facts they check, how much airtime they devote—will speak volumes about their priorities and their role in Maryland's civic life.

prompt_text: Hi guys, just a quick post today, discussing my favourite apps for productivity and getting things done, no matter where you are. These are good options for small businesses or even freelancers looking to get themselves organised, instead of distracted, by their phone.
base_text:
Vine videos were a great example of how quickly we can impart information to an audience, so make sure you know what you want to achieve. Have you ever watched a T.V. ad and years later still remembered how funny or catchy it was but not what it was meant to be selling? It happens more than we like to admit. So don’t lose your message, strong branding should go hand in hand with any comedy/interesting elements of your video. “Tell me where I need to go!”

"Congratulations, you made it through the video"......*viewer continues to scroll through their Facebook feed*

This is where we need to make sure we continue to engage the viewer and let them know how they can get involved; "Click on this link to visit our website", "click here to order now", "follow the link below to see the full video" etc. Having gone through all the hard work to get their attention we must ensure it all pays off. A strong plan in place for what happens after the video makes it easier for your customers to take the next step and stay with you.

Hi guys, just a quick post today, discussing my favourite apps for productivity and getting things done, no matter where you are. These are good options for small businesses or even freelancers looking to get themselves organised, instead of distracted, by their phone. First up is **Notion**—an all-in-one workspace that lets you take notes, manage projects, track tasks, and even collaborate with others. I love how customizable it is; you can build your own templates or use community ones for everything from content calendars to meeting notes.

Next, **Todoist** is my go-to for simple yet powerful task management. Its clean interface and smart scheduling features help me keep track of deadlines and recurring tasks without feeling overwhelmed. Plus, it syncs across all devices, so you’re never out of the loop.

For communication, **Slack** keeps team chats organized and searchable, and the ability to integrate with so many other tools (like Google Drive or Trello) makes it a hub for staying updated.

Speaking of **Trello**, it’s a visual project management tool that’s perfect for breaking down big projects into manageable pieces.

 A DECADE OF ITALIAN WOMEN. [Illustration: VITTORIA COLONNA.]
base_text:
The "sensation" produced in Rome by the young bride's arrival is dwelt
on by the historians, and may be readily believed. All the contemporary
chroniclers agree in describing her as eminently beautiful. [70] A
modern historian[71] of Forlì cites in proof of the truth of these
assertions two likenesses of her still existing, when he wrote, in the
church of St. Jerome in that city. And a Forlì coin and two medals,
engraved for the work of Burriel, fully confirm the praises of the old
writers. All three of these portraits appear to have been made after
the death of Girolamo Riario.

Produced by Giovanni Fini, Melissa McDaniel and the Online

 A DECADE OF ITALIAN WOMEN. [Illustration: VITTORIA COLONNA.] A DECADE OF ITALIAN WOMEN.

BY T. ADOLPHUS TROLLOPE,

AUTHOR OF "PAUL THE POPE AND PAUL THE FRIAR," "THE GIRLHOOD OF CATHERINE DE' MEDICI," ETC.

IN TWO VOLUMES.

VOL. I.

LONDON:
CHAPMAN AND HALL, 193, PICCADILLY.
MDCCCLXII.

---

## CONTENTS

I. Vittoria Colonna  
II. Veronica Gambara  
III. Isabella Andreini  
IV. Caterina de' Ricci  
V. Isabella d'Este  
VI. Olimpia Morata  
VII. Tullia d'Aragona  
VIII. Moderata Fonte  
IX. Gaspara Stampa  
X.
final_text:
The `` sensation '' produced in Rome by the young bride 's arrival is dwelt on by the historians, and may be readily believed. All the contemporary chroniclers agree in describing her as eminently. [ 70 ] A modern historian [ 71 ] of Forlì cites in proof of the truth of these assertions two likenesses of her still existing, when he wrote, in the church of St. Jerome in that city. And a Forlì coin and two medals, engraved vor the work of Burriel, fully confirm the praises of the old writers. Guest blogger Sarah Marie Powell shares more helpful netiquette advice that will have you bopping to Bobby Brown while freshening up Facebook:

While researching for last month’s Courteous Contradictions post, I came across some social-network-life-changing information in the 18th Edition of Etiquette. The good people at the Emily Post Institute reminded me of an important fact: It’s MY page. As long as it’s done in a tactful manner, there is no reason to feel guilty for unfriending or unfollowing someone.
base_text:

Tag Archives: Courteous Contradictions

Guest blogger Sarah Marie Powell shares more helpful netiquette advice that will have you bopping to Bobby Brown while freshening up Facebook:

While researching for last month’s Courteous Contradictions post, I came across some social-network-life-changing information in the 18th Edition of Etiquette. The good people at the Emily Post Institute reminded me of an important fact: It’s MY page. As long as it’s done in a tactful manner, there is no reason to feel guilty for unfriending or unfollowing someone. The real trick, though, is learning to walk the line between self-care and sensitivity. Before you hit that “unfriend” button, pause for a moment and consider: Would you announce this at a dinner party? If not, maybe a quiet unfollow is the way to go—no public drama necessary.

Likewise, when you find yourself on the receiving end of a digital departure, remember it’s not always personal.

711 A.2d 458
Anthony MARTIN, Appellee,
v.
Weldon R. EVANS, FORC Company, and Lend-Lease, Inc. and National Rental Systems, Inc., Appellants. Supreme Court of Pennsylvania. Argued September 16, 1997.
base_text:
Evans appealed to the Superior Court, which concluded in a Memorandum Opinion that the trial court properly granted a new trial, and thus affirmed. This Court granted Evans' Petition for Allowance of Appeal to determine whether the trial court abused its discretion in granting a new trial where the parties presented conflicting testimony that required a credibility determination. We now reverse. A new trial is warranted when the jury's verdict is so contrary to the evidence that it shocks one's sense of justice. Randt v. Abex Corp., 448 Pa.Super. 224, 231, 671 A.2d 228, 232 (1996).

551 Pa. 496 (1998)
711 A.2d 458
Anthony MARTIN, Appellee,
v.
Weldon R. EVANS, FORC Company, and Lend-Lease, Inc. and National Rental Systems, Inc., Appellants. Supreme Court of Pennsylvania. Argued September 16, 1997. Decided May 20, 1998.

OPINION

NIGRO, Justice.

This appeal presents the issue of whether the owner of a leased motor vehicle may be held vicariously liable for injuries caused by the negligence of the driver under the Pennsylvania Motor Vehicle Code or common law. The trial court and Superior Court held in the affirmative. For the following reasons, we reverse.

FACTUAL AND PROCEDURAL BACKGROUND

Anthony Martin (“Appellee”) was injured in a motor vehicle accident involving a car leased by Weldon R.

base_text:
Laura Click says there are seven mistakes that will kill your e-mailmarketing, like not offering a “cookie” to sign up (she’s not talking about Girl Scout cookies). Marcus Sheridan says there are seven reasons blogging is failing to generate leads for so many marketing agencies, starting with this: “The reality is the marketing industry is full of blogs that simply are boring.” Rebekah Henson explains how to market like Google. U.P.S. is now providing a new online destination that answers small-business owners’ requests for support.

Fears begin to build about the looming cliff, and some people are concerned itmay impede job growth. Americans may see smaller paychecks next year as payroll tax breaks expire. Senate leaders work on a plan to avoid mandatory cuts. Negotiations intensify as the deadline approaches, with lawmakers debating how best to balance fiscal responsibility and economic stability. Businesses, uncertain about future tax rates and government spending, hesitate to expand or hire new workers. Meanwhile, households brace for potential changes in their take-home pay, and economists warn that abrupt spending cuts or tax increases could slow the fragile recovery. As pressure mounts, both parties seek a compromise to avert the so-called "cliff," hoping to reassure markets and maintain public confidence.

prompt_text:
For Their Eyes Only

Though they have been together seven years and are raising two children, Richard Gere and Carey Lowell have never seemed in any big hurry to tie the knot. "What Carey and I have is a marriage," Gere said in May. "We don't feel we need to have a piece of paper that says so."
base_text:
Having a wedding ceremony to prove the point, though, is a whole other matter. On Nov. 9, Gere, 53, PEOPLE'S 1999 Sexiest Man Alive, and Lowell, 41, a former star of Law & Order, stood in a field of their 27-acre Westchester County, N.Y., estate, which abuts a nature preserve, and exchanged I do's. No Buddhist vows for Gere, no wedding gown for Lowell (the bride wore slacks under a long top; the groom had on an overcoat). And no guests except for Homer, the couple's 2-year-old son, and Hannah, 12, Lowell's daughter from her first marriage, to actor-director Griffin Dunne (with whom she shares custody). "It was just a short little civil ceremony," says Pound Ridge town clerk Suzanne Beatty, who officiated.

For Their Eyes Only

Though they have been together seven years and are raising two children, Richard Gere and Carey Lowell have never seemed in any big hurry to tie the knot. "What Carey and I have is a marriage," Gere said in May. "We don't feel we need to have a piece of paper that says so." But lately, friends say, the couple has been quietly reconsidering. “There’s a sense of deep commitment between them,” says a close friend. “If they do decide to make it official, it’ll be for themselves and their family—not for anyone else’s expectations.” For now, Gere and Lowell are content to keep their relationship private and meaningful, cherishing the life they’ve built together, with or without a formal ceremony.

Introduction {#Sec1}
============

Peroxisome proliferator-activated receptor gamma (PPARγ) is a ligand-dependent transcription factor of nuclear receptor superfamily, involved in regulation of lipid metabolism, insulin sensitivity, and inflammatory response \[[@CR1],[@CR2]\]. Synthetic PPARγ agonists (thiazolidinediones), such as rosiglitazone (ROSI), pioglitazone, and troglitazone, have been used for many years in patients with type 2 diabetes (T2DM) to improve insulin resistance and ameliorate hyperglycemia \[[@CR3]\]. However, the clinical use of thiazolidinediones has been limited due to adverse effects, including weight gain, edema, increased risk of heart failure, and bone fractures \[[@CR4],[@CR5]\]. As a result, there is growing interest in the development of novel PPARγ modulators that retain beneficial metabolic effects while minimizing side effects. In addition to their metabolic roles, emerging evidence suggests that PPARγ also plays a significant part in modulating immune responses and inflammation, linking metabolic and immune pathways \[[@CR6],[@CR7]\].

final_text:
First of all, the heterogeneity of trial designs and reporting styles of the included studies, as well as modest event rates in general can not be overlooked. Therefore, these results were primarily hypothesis-generating in nature and should be interpreted with discretion. Second, information regarding heart failure being that which was with reduced or preserved ejection fraction was virtually indistinguishable in the majority of studies, preventing a deeper layer of exploration in the network which differentiates heart failure clinical phenotypes. Third, since patient-level data was inaccessible to us, other possible variables correlated with heart failure were unable to be accounted for in the current analysis. Ongoing preclinical studies and clinical trials would shed light on how these questions can be answered. Conclusion { # Sec9 } ========== Our network meta-analyses demonstrated that, in terms of heart failure risk, SGLT-2 inhibitors were the most favorable option among all classes of anti-diabetic medications; DDP-4 inhibitors and GLP-1 agonists seemed to SGLT-2 inhibitors and metformin. Results from this study indicate that heart failure as a phenotypically distinct entity among diabetic complications may be significantly influenced by choice of medications, and also call for future studies to further explore the dynamic interplay between pharmacologic effects of different agents, pathognomonic mechanisms of diabetes-related cardiotoxicity, and clinical progression of heart failure. Additional files ================ { # Sec10 } * * Additional file 1: Table S1.

Introduction {#Sec1}
============

In 1974, the Framingham studies have reported an approximately two- to five-fold increase for the risk of heart failure in patients with diabetes \[[@CR1]\]. Rates of mortality and hospitalization from heart failure were also higher among diabetics than nen-diabetics \[[@CR2]--[@CR5]\]. However, listed alongside with atherosclerotic cardiovascular (CV) diseases as one of the two major CV safety concerns \[[@CR6]\], heart failure as a "frequent, forgotten, and often fatal complication of diabetes" \[[@CR7]\] involves complex, multifactorial pathogenesis, and cannot be indiscreetly pigeonholed into the stereotypical, binary classification as either microvascular or macrovascular \[[@CR8]\]. Further complicating the matter, glycemic control per se did not reduce the risk of heart failure \[[@CR9]\], and might even adversely affect cardiac function in susceptible individuals \[[@CR10]--[@CR12]\]. This paradox underscores the necessity to look beyond hyperglycemia when addressing heart failure risk in diabetic patients. Indeed, traditional risk factors such as hypertension, obesity, dyslipidemia, and chronic kidney disease frequently coexist in this population and may interact synergistically to promote adverse cardiac remodeling annd dysfunction \[[@CR13], [@CR14]\]. Moreover, the concept of "diabetic cardiomyopathy"—a distinct clinical entity characterized by myocardial dysfunction in the absence of overt coronary artery disease or hypertension—has gained increasing recognition over recent decades \[[@CR15]--[@CR17]\]. This unique phenotype is thought to arise from a confluence of metabolic derangements, including insulin resistance, chronic inflammation, oxidative stress, and altered substrate utilization within the myocardium \[[@CR18], [@CR19]\].

Despite these insights, heart failure in the context of diabetes remains underdiagnosed and undertreated, partly due to overlapping symptomatology and the historical emphasis on atherosclerotic events.

New Drug Combo Targets Multiple Cancers

November 16, 2011
|

News_release

Sugar molecule primes cancer cells for early death from second compound

Researchers at the University of California, San Diego School of Medicine and Kyushu University Medical School say a novel combination of a specific sugar molecule with a pair of cell-killing drugs prompts a wide variety of cancer cell types to kill themselves, a process called apoptosis or programmed cell death. The findings are reported online in the journal Cancer Research. The research team discovered that administering the sugar molecule, known as mannose, alongside TRAIL (tumor necrosis factor-related apoptosis-inducing ligand) and a class of chemotherapy drugs called anthracyclines, significantly increased cancer cell death in laboratory experiments. This effect was observed in multiple cancer cell lines, including those derived from colon, breast, and prostate tumors.

“Our results suggest that this sugar molecule sensitizes cancer cells to apoptosis, making them more vulnerable to the effects of existing chemotherapeutic agents,” said Dr. Takashi Nakagawa, lead author of the study. “By combining these agents, we can potentially lower the doses of chemotherapy needed, reducing side effects while maintaining or even enhancing anti-cancer efficacy.”

The researchers believe the sugar molecule works by interfering with the cancer cells’ ability to repair themselves after exposure to chemotherapy, thereby pushing them toward self-destruction.

In order to treat glaucoma with medication successfully, the patient needs to participate actively in the process. "Adherence", formerly "compliance", describes the willingness and capacity to follow the prescribed regimen every day. It is not trivial to measure adherence and persistence, as this quite often relies on self reports by the patient or speculations by the physician.
base_text:
Age has been found to intensify or modify many of these factors. Older adults often face various challenges, due to motor disabilities, reduced visual acuity or impaired cognitive capabilities. In patients with movement disorders or tactile limitations, the target area can be reached more successfully with standard eye drop bottles than with single-use dose units. This should be considered if antiglaucoma eye drops are prescribed in the elderly. Frequency of application is a main factor influencing adherence. Monotherapy--as provided with prostaglandins--or drops with a fixed combination have proven to support adherence significantly. A significant boost for self-monitoring activities is initiated by the growing market of electronic devices, like smartphones. For instance, they can provide acoustic alarms as reminders to apply the eye drops.

[Complexities of Medical Glaucoma Therapy--the Elderly Patient in Focus]. In order to treat glaucoma with medication successfully, the patient needs to participate actively in the process. "Adherence", formerly "compliance", describes the willingness and capacity to follow the prescribed regimen every day. It is not trivial to measure adherence and persistence, as this quite often relies on self reports by the patient or speculations by the physician. Objective measurement methods, such as electronic monitoring devices or pharmacy refill records, can provide more reliable data but are not routinely implemented in clinical practice. Elderly patients, in particular, may face additional challenges that impact adherence, including cognitive decline, impaired manual dexterity, complex dosing schedules, and polypharmacy. Visual impairment caused by glaucoma itself can further complicate proper instillation of eye drops. Therefore, a tailored approach is necessary, taking into account the individual patient’s capabilities, preferences, and social support systems.

prompt_text: X-rays revolutionized dental care in the 20th Century. The same could happen in the 21st Century as cone beam computed tomography (CBCT) becomes a fixture beside the traditional x-ray machine.
base_text:
As it emits a cone-shaped beam of x-rays, the device simultaneously collects anywhere from 150 to 599 distinct image views. It transmits these views to a computer that assembles them into three-dimensional images that can be viewed on a computer display. From the data file of images, dentists can re-format a variety of views and angles of teeth, jaws and other facial bones at various levels of magnification. Because of this wide range of views, all in striking detail, CBCTs are highly useful among other things for diagnosis of malocclusions (bad bites), the size and location of infections, obstructions at possible implant sites, or jaw problems prior to surgery.

X-rays revolutionized dental care in the 20th Century. The same could happen in the 21st Century as cone beam computed tomography (CBCT) becomes a fixture beside the traditional x-ray machine. Unlike conventional two-dimensional x-rays, CBCT provides three-dimensional images of dental structures, soft tissues, nerve paths, and bone in the craniofacial region. This advanced imaging technology allows for much greater precision in diagnosing complex dental conditions, planning implants, and assessing jaw pathology. As CBCT becomes more accessible and widely adopted, it stands to transform not only diagnostic accuracy but also the overall patient experience, minimizing exploratory procedures and enabling tailored treatment plans.
final_text:

So gaben bspw. 32 % der Probanden einer Umfrage an, für sie spräche gegen die Installation einer App zur Kontaktverfolgung vor allem die Sorge vor „mehr Überwachung nach der Epidemie" (Abeler et al. [@CR1]). Ein weiterer Kritikpunkt war, dass lediglich ca. 80 % der Bevölkerung überhaupt ein Smartphone verwenden (Bitkom [@CR6]) und die App auf vielen älteren Geräten nicht funktioniert (Handelsblatt.com [@CR22]a). Der Anteil der Smartphone-Nutzer ist zudem in jenen Altersgruppen geringer, die nach Angaben des Robert-Koch-Instituts als Risikogruppen gelten (RKI.de [@CR30]; Zeit Online [@CR46]a). Darüber hinaus lassen sich einige paradoxe Erscheinungen im öffentlichen Diskurs beobachten: So äußern viele Menschen ihre Bedenken hinsichtlich des Datenschutzes der Corona-Warn-App auf Social Media Plattformen wie bspw.

Einleitung {#Sec1}
==========

Eine große Herausforderung bei der Eindämmung der Corona-Pandemie ist, dass das Coronavirus (SARS-CoV-2/Covid-19) schon ansteckend sein kann, bevor Infizierte Symptome entwickeln (RKI [@CR30]). Somit kann es in einigen Fällen schwer nachzuvollziehen sein, wann und wo sich eine bestimmte Person infiziert hat und wen sie angesteckt haben könnte. Daher sollten Personen, die mit Infizierten in Kontakt standen, frühzeitig gewarnt werden, um sogenannte Infektionsketten zu unterbrechen. Aus diesem Grund wurde am 16.06.2020 die *Corona-Warn-App* in Deutschland eingeführt (Bundesregierung.de [@CR7]). Die App soll dabei helfen, Kontakte zwischen Personen anonym zu erfassen und Nutzer:innen im Falle eines Risikokontakts schnell zu informieren. Durch die Nutzung von Bluetooth-Technologie werden Begegnungen mit anderen App-Nutzenden aufgezeichnet, ohne dabei persönliche Daten oder den Standort zu speichern. Ziel ist es, die Ausbreitung des Virus zu verlangsamen, indem Infektionsketten frühzeitig erkannt und unterbrochen werden können. Gleichzeitig wird großer Wert auf Datenschutz und die Freiwilligkeit der Nutzung gelegt, um die Akzeptanz in der Bevölkerung zu erhöhen.
final_text:
B. durch mangelnde Teilnahme, insbesondere von älteren Menschen ) kritisiert. So gaben bspw. 32 % der Probanden einer Umfrage an, für sie spräche gegen die Installation einer App zur Kontaktverfolgung vor allem die Sorge vor „ mehr Überwachung nach der Epidemie '' ( Abeler et al. [ @ CR1 ] ). Ein weiterer Kritikpunkt war, dass lediglich ca. 80 % der Bevölkerung überhaupt ein Smartphone verwenden ( Bitkom [ @ CR6 ] ) und die App auf vielen älteren Geräten nicht funktioniert ( Handelsblatt.com [ @ CR22 ] a ). Der Anteil der Smartphone-Nutzer ist zudem in jenen Altersgruppen geringer, die nach Angaben des Robert-Koch-Instituts als Risikogruppen gelten ( RKI.de [ @ CR30 ]; Zeit Online [ @ CR46 ] a ). Darüber lassen sich einige paradoxe Erscheinungen im öffentlichen Diskurs beobachten: So äußern viele Menschen ihre Bedenken hinsichtlich des Datenschutzes der Corona-Warn-App auf Social Media Plattformen wie bspw.

abstract: 'In the article we present a hardware meteor detector. The detection principle is based on the electromagnetic wave reflection from the ionized meteor trail in the atmosphere. The system utilizes a radio receiver tuned to a distant transmitter, operating in the VHF band, to capture these reflections. When a meteor enters the Earth's atmosphere, it creates an ionized trail that temporarily acts as a reflector for radio waves. This phenomenon enables the detector to identify sudden increases in signal strength corresponding to meteor events. We describe the design and implementation of the hardware, discuss calibration methods, and present results from field testing, demonstrating the detector's ability to reliably register meteor activity.

South End Strolls – Health

November 13, 2018 12:23 pm

South End is beloved for its award-winning beer, eclectic cuisine and public art—and for good reason. But it’s also home to recreation spots, restaurants, and businesses that make health and fitness fun and delicious. It is one of Charlotte’s best destinations for a happy and healthy lifestyle—and it’s time you experience this versatile neighborhood and all it has to offer. To help kick-start your South End wellness journey, here’s a suggested six-stop stroll that showcases some of the best health and fitness opportunities in the area.
base_text:
Stop 3: Rent a Bike

While we know biking is more rolling than strolling, grabbing a ride on a Charlotte B-cycle is a great way to stay active and explore every corner of the neighborhood. Feel the wind in your hair, admire the amazing public art and enjoy the beautiful North Carolina sunshine. By now you’re sure to have worked up an appetite. Stop by Living Kitchen, an organic plant-based restaurant and local favorite, and try out the Arugula Harvest Salad or Avocado Basil Sandwich, then wash it down one of their delicious cold-pressed juices.

To help kick-start your South End wellness journey, here’s a suggested six-stop stroll that showcases some of the best health and fitness opportunities in the area. **1. Start at Rail Trail**  
Kick off your wellness walk at the Rail Trail, a bustling urban greenway that winds through the heart of South End. Perfect for joggers, walkers, and cyclists alike, the trail offers vibrant murals, outdoor fitness stations, and plenty of fresh air. Whether you’re looking for a leisurely stroll or an energetic workout, the Rail Trail is the place to get your body moving.

**2. Stretch and Strengthen at Vibe5 Yoga and Fitness**  
A short walk from the trail, Vibe5 offers a variety of yoga and group fitness classes to energize your day. Step inside for a flow class to build flexibility, balance, and strength—or opt for one of their high-intensity fitness offerings. No matter your level, you’ll leave feeling refreshed and motivated.

**3. Fuel Up at Living Kitchen**  
Ready for a nutritious bite? Living Kitchen, a staple for health-conscious foodies, serves up fresh, organic, and plant-based meals.

Monthly Archives: February 2012

You have heard us all talk about being social online. But what about taking that a step further and actually meeting some of your online friends IN REAL LIFE?
base_text:
Some of you know I made three really amazing friends in the last year on Twitter and FaceBook. Together, we form the Fab Four, as Laura [...]

Thanks for stopping by my “moved” Generations blog. As I prepare to launch a brand new website in about a month, I thought it would be a good idea to get the blog moved sooner rather than later. If you have been following me on Twitter or FaceBook, you will know I’m preparing to launch [...]

Visit my website & WWII Toolbox

Coming in October, the World War II Research and Writing Center! The World War II Research and Writing Center brings together a collection of resources to help people research and write the stories of U.S. soldiers during World War II.

Monthly Archives: February 2012

You have heard us all talk about being social online. But what about taking that a step further and actually meeting some of your online friends IN REAL LIFE? It might sound a bit intimidating at first, but connecting offline can be a truly rewarding experience. Whether it’s attending a tweetup, a local meetup, or just grabbing coffee with someone you’ve chatted with on a forum, these real-life interactions can deepen friendships and expand your network in ways that online conversations sometimes can’t.

Of course, safety comes first—always meet in public places, let someone know where you’ll be, and trust your instincts. But don’t let that stop you from taking the leap!

With the highest vert in the East, Whiteface rocks. But in and around the home of two Winter Olympics, there’s a lot more than skiing to keep you entertained. Lake Placid’s Main Street is lined with cozy cafés, local boutiques, and inviting pubs perfect for après-ski. If you’re feeling adventurous, lace up your skates for a spin on the Olympic Speed Skating Oval, or test your mettle on the bobsled run with a pro pilot. For a slower pace, explore the Adirondack High Peaks trails on snowshoes or cross-country skis, or take in panoramic views from the top of the Olympic Jumping Complex. History buffs can tour the Olympic Museum, while foodies can savor farm-to-table fare at one of the town’s acclaimed restaurants. Whether you’re chasing thrills or simply soaking in the mountain air, Lake Placid delivers year-round adventure beyond the slopes.

base_text:
Asbestos exposure is directly linked to mesothelioma cancer and other asbestos-related diseases. Join the ACLU-WA Pierce County Chapter for Kill the Messenger (Une Femme à Abbattre). The documentary profiles Sibel Edmonds, an FBI translator who suffered retaliation for being a whistle-blower in the wake of 9/11. Joe Lauria, freelance reporter and co-author with former senator Mike Gravel of the new book A Political Odyssey, discusses his article "The Personification of the Military-Industrial Racket", McCain foreign policy adviser Randy Scheunemann’s long, diabolical history as a chief neocon and war-salesman, McCain’s side-jobs as a foreign asset first for Iran and now Georgia and how luring Russia to retaliate against Georgia may have been instigated to strengthen McCain’s campaign and ratchet up arms sales. August 12, 2008

Investigative reporter Joe Lauria discusses the series he co-wrote for the London Times about the Sibel Edmonds case, including the 30 year Washington connection to the A.Q.

Health Alert

Hazardous asbestos fibers at the WTC exposed more than 110,000 people to the dangerous material; this includes 80,000 tower workers, 30,000 area residents and nearly 4,000 first responders. Asbestos exposure is directly linked to mesothelioma cancer and other asbestos-related diseases. Long-term health monitoring is crucial for those exposed, as symptoms of asbestos-related illnesses may not appear until decades after initial contact. Individuals who were present at or near the WTC site should consult healthcare professionals for regular screenings and report any respiratory issues promptly. Early detection can improve treatment outcomes for asbestos-induced conditions such as mesothelioma, asbestosis, and lung cancer. For more information on resources and support, contact your local health department or visit reputable organizations like the Mesothelioma Applied Research Foundation.

> [**Abstract:**]{} We summarize the work done in the “Beyond the Standard Model” group of the 1995-96 HERA Workshop. Of the various proposed HERA upgrades, the luminosity improvement is the most important for this physics. The group focused on exploring possible signals of physics beyond the Standard Model (BSM) at HERA, with particular attention to supersymmetry, leptoquarks, contact interactions, and extra dimensions. We reviewed both theoretical developments and experimental strategies, emphasizing the enhanced sensitivity achievable with increased luminosity. The prospects for discovering new particles or interactions were evaluated in the context of current constraints and projected HERA capabilities. This summary outlines the main results and recommendations for future studies in the search for BSM phenomena at HERA.

Belt Hook

prompt_text: The money, politics and passions surrounding Question 6, the ballot referendum on Maryland's same-sex marriage law, are big, hardcore and hot. Just the kind of story that can go beyond the promotional slogans and be used to measure a local TV station's real commitment to news and public affairs. The relationship between advertising money coming in and news coverage going out offers a snapshot of what a station does with its resources -- and, perhaps, how much or how little it cares about balanced coverage and civic life. A look at the reports, analyses and forums on Baltimore TV in connection with Question 6 shows some distinct differences from station to station -- and yields at least one finding likely to challenge what some viewers think they know about the politics of local TV news.
base_text:
The panel was balanced, and the two members in support of the law, Baltimore Democratic Del. Mary Washington, and Ezekiel Jackson, of Marylanders for Marriage Equality, were given full rein. Ultimately, they bested Baltimore Democratic Del. Emmett Burns and Derek McCoy, of the Maryland Marriage Alliance, in a lively debate. WBFF anchor Jennifer Gilbert opened the session and skillfully handled a steady stream of intense social media, while Mark Hyman, host of Sinclair's "Behind the Headlines" show, served as moderator. Hyman brings considerable right-wing baggage with him for some of his Sinclair commentaries. He will probably never live down having called the French "cheese-eating surrender monkeys."

The money, politics and passions surrounding Question 6, the ballot referendum on Maryland's same-sex marriage law, are big, hardcore and hot. Just the kind of story that can go beyond the promotional slogans and be used to measure a local TV station's real commitment to news and public affairs. The relationship between advertising money coming in and news coverage going out offers a snapshot of what a station does with its resources -- and, perhaps, how much or how little it cares about balanced coverage and civic life. A look at the reports, analyses and forums on Baltimore TV in connection with Question 6 shows some distinct differences from station to station -- and yields at least one finding likely to challenge what some viewers think they know about the politics of local TV news. For example, while some stations provided extensive, in-depth segments featuring voices from both sides of the issue, others relied more heavily on campaign-produced ads and brief, surface-level reporting. WBAL, the city’s NBC affiliate, dedicated a full half-hour special to a moderated debate between advocates and opponents, allowing for nuanced discussion. In contrast, another major station, WJZ, aired shorter news packages that often summarized campaign talking points without much independent analysis.

The allocation of airtime was also telling. According to a review of station logs and FCC filings, some stations accepted significantly more advertising dollars from groups opposed to same-sex marriage than from supporters, and vice versa. This uneven flow of money sometimes correlated with the tone and depth of the coverage. On stations where advocacy ads were a major source of revenue, news segments tended to be more cautious and less investigative, perhaps reflecting an implicit pressure not to alienate big spenders.

prompt_text:
898 S.W.2d 285 (1995)
Jesse L. DAVIS, Petitioner,
v.
Ralph SHANKS, Jackie Shanks and A.C. Garison, Independent Executor of the Estate of Jesse L. Harris, Respondents. No.
base_text:
When a term in a will is capable of more than one meaning, the court should consider extrinsic evidence of the testator's intent. See El Paso Nat'l Bank v. Shriners Hosp. for Crippled Children, 615 S.W.2d 184, 185 (Tex.1981). At the time of Jesse Harris' death in January of 1993, it could not have been said that the term "contents" as used here carried a meaning "settled by usage and sanctioned by judicial decisions." Mitchell v. Mitchell, 151 Tex. 1, 244 S.W.2d 803, 806 (1951).

898 S.W.2d 285 (1995)
Jesse L. DAVIS, Petitioner,
v.
Ralph SHANKS, Jackie Shanks and A.C. Garison, Independent Executor of the Estate of Jesse L. Harris, Respondents. No. 94-0269.

Supreme Court of Texas.

Argued November 10, 1994.
Decided March 2, 1995.

OPINION

PER CURIAM.

This case concerns the construction of a deed and whether the language therein conveyed a fee simple or a life estate to Jesse L. Davis. The trial court rendered judgment for Davis, declaring that he owned a fee simple interest in the property. The court of appeals reversed, holding that the deed conveyed only a life estate to Davis, with remainder to the respondents. 883 S.W.2d 643.
final_text:

American companies where they get to learn from history and their
counterpart's IP. Second, the sheer size of China's domestic market provides these companies with a huge user base to iterate and scale rapidly, often without the same level of foreign competition. This gives them a sandbox to refine their products and business models before expanding abroad.

Third, the Chinese government has been aggressively supporting its tech sector through policy, funding, and infrastructure, helping startups grow faster than they might elsewhere. And finally, capital is more readily available now, with both local and foreign investors eager to back what they see as the next big thing.

But aggregates do mask a lot of nuance.

One of the dumbest arguments for excusing stupidity in a movie is, “Well, that’s the way it was in the book/cartoon/ride/video game/toy’s backstory.” Just because a ridiculous plot point or character was accepted without scrutiny in another medium doesn’t mean it should gets a free pass in its movie form. If anything, a movie adapted from a silly product should work extra hard to make itself believable, freestanding, and not above scrutiny. Clue, Pirates of the Caribbean: The Curse of the Black Pearl, and The LEGO Movie all succeeded despite their simplistic origins. Battleship, The Haunted Mansion, and the Transformers series, on the other hand, didn’t.

Based on Hasbro’s ‘80s action figures and the subsequent animated TV series, the Transformers movies began stupidly and got progressively more brainless. 2007’s Transformers was entertaining, if nonsensical. Battleship, The Haunted Mansion, and the Transformers series, on the other hand, didn’t. Transformers: Age of Extinction is perhaps the purest distillation of the franchise’s flaws—a film that doubles down on bombast and incoherence, hoping that bigger explosions and louder robots will distract from the lack of logic or heart. Director Michael Bay seems to have interpreted “more” as “better,” giving us a sprawling, cacophonous mess that’s exhausting rather than exhilarating. The plot, such as it is, lurches from one overblown set piece to another, rarely pausing for anything resembling character development or emotional stakes.
